verdicts.reason: URL regex silently truncates valid URLs; malformed percent-encoding crashes decode logic; localStorage not used despite offline claims; CSP allows unsafe-inline; port parsing accepts non-numeric input; bulk URL extraction loses data on edge cases.

CSP policy allows unsafe-inline and connect-src *, enabling XSS via injected styles/scripts and data exfiltration; password field rendered in DOM; URL regex extraction vulnerable to ReDoS; no input validation on rebuild fields.

Injected accessibility override uses !important to force illegible 19px base font and max() constraints that break the intentional retro-terminal design, creating visual chaos and defeating the project's stated aesthetic.
No README.md exists; tool features (Dissect, Resolve, Bulk) are undocumented; keyboard shortcuts and advanced features are invisible to first-time users and AI agents.

Solves real recurring need: developers and QA engineers regularly need to inspect URL structure, decode parameters, and resolve relative URLs. This tool eliminates context-switching to browser devtools or online parsers.
Signature move: live-rebuild widget that lets you edit URL components and watch the result update in real-time. Memorable, shareable, unreasonably good at one thing.

Deliverable violates documented KEEP lesson: 'String.prototype.split(x).join(y) replaces .replace(/x/g, y) when x is "' — code uses .replace(/g) regex patterns instead of split/join for quote escaping and URL trimming.

Injected accessibility override uses !important to force illegible 0.38rem fonts back to 15px minimum, breaking the intentional visual hierarchy and creating jarring, inconsistent typography that confuses information priority.

Solves a real, recurring pain point (cron expression comprehension) with zero friction — paste, read, done. Second user exists: anyone managing scheduled tasks.

UUID v1 generation sets multicast bit on wrong byte, corrupting node field; v7 variant byte construction is incorrect; normalizeUUID silently accepts invalid 32-char hex without dashes, masking input errors.

CSP policy is fatally permissive (connect-src *; unsafe-inline), enabling XSS and data exfiltration; user input rendered via innerHTML without sanitization; clipboard API misuse exposes data to malicious scripts.

Injected accessibility override uses !important to force illegible 0.55rem fonts back to 15px minimum, but core interaction patterns remain confusing: empty state shows no guidance, generator output lacks affordance clarity, bulk mode toggle is buried, and error messaging is cryptic.
No README.md exists; the 'Guide' button links to a non-existent file, leaving users and AI agents without discoverable documentation of features, input formats, or keyboard shortcuts.
Real recurring use case: developers regularly inspect UUID structure, validate format, and generate test UUIDs. This tool solves a genuine workflow gap.
UUID inspector is a competent implementation but indistinguishable from crontab.guru / regex101 / jsonformatter — a straightforward decode-and-display tool with no signature move or memorable angle.
No documented lessons violated. Single-file HTML, zero external deps, pure JS, CSP headers, accessibility overrides, and no repeat anti-patterns detected.
